# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Create Release Workflow for Python Application
on: [push]  # Triggers on `push` events but only at specified branches (like `master`) - https://help.github.com/articles/about-branches-and-merges/#events-that-trigger-workflows 👈 click here for more details
jobs:  # This is the list of jobs which are executed by GitHub Actions (https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro-team@latest/actions)💻 clicking on 'more' to see other available options, such as concurrency 👈 https://help.github.com/articles/concurrency-(immutability-)and-persistence
  build_publish:   # Job name must be unique under one workflow and job id is necessary (https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro-team@latest)👈 click on 'more' to see other options, such as stages or jobs within the same steps run in parallel
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  # Use this Docker image for Linux 🐳 https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu   (this will automatically install Node.js and Yarn)💻 clicking on 'more' to see other options, such as matrix configuration -> docker runner version
    steps:     # Array of actions or commands that can be run locally with the `run` keyword  https://github.com/actions/runner-images#available-images   (this will automatically install Python and NodeJS)💻 clicking on 'more' to see other options, such as cache dependencies
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # Check out your repository using GitHub Actions 👈 https://github.com/actions  // If you want this step only when a specific branch is specified (e.g., `branches` or just master) then use the following configuration
        with:   # Specifies what parameters to pass through actions⚠️, and which inputs are provided on runtime(default parameter set for GitHub Actions )💻 clicking here https://docs.github.com/en  // (click 'more' option above) 👈
          fetch-depth: 0   # Shallow clones should be disabled for a better relevancy of analysis⚠️, but still can contain large file differences if present! If so run shallow clone with `FetchDepth` set to greater than the number of commits considered relevant.
      - name: Setup Python Environment # You have to specify actions that should be run in this step before others    // ⚠️'more options below under 'with', above it is just setting up our python environment as per configuration specified within Dockerfile or by using `workflow_call` function.
        uses: cntrump/setup-python@v1 | you can replace with your custom Python version manager if available in the registry, and run below step when fetching repo  #👈 https://github.com (click 'more' option above) 🔄 running actions as part of previous job
        id: setup_python   // Unique Id for this specific workflow-job -> If there are multiple steps in a `run`, specify unique name or use parallelism keyword to make the sequential execution.  In our case I used 'setup' and later can call it inside any next step by '. ./.envs/python_version setup'.
      # More actions will go here based on your needs 🔄 - (e.g., building, testing with pytest)💻 clicking below: https://github.com  // more options as per requirement for the tasks above can be provided in this block of code inside 'runs-on' section
      # Here we have to run commands on our Python project and then publish it into npm/PyPI when a tag is pushed because GitHub Actions does not provide publishing functionality through .yaml file. It requires manual command execution as mentioned above with the `run` keyword in 'steps'.   // 👈 https://docs.github.com  (click below option: more)
      - name: Build Python Application # This will build your project🔄⚠️, when not used explicitly but still needs to be defined and also can specify the actions within this step that should run in specific cases/when required by our use case. In above scenario its building of application based on requirement
        run: |   # More details about each action will go inside here as per your requirements like setup dependencies, install packages etc..🔄 https://github.com  // more options for actions can be provided in this block using `run` keyword or we could use shell command to build our application if required
          echo "Building Python Application" # Echo is used instead of print statement so as not disturb GitHub's output and make the process automated through commands, you provide all necessary details inside here. If any error occurs during this step then it should fail automatically🔄 https://github.com  // more options for actions can be provided in block using `run` keyword
      - name: Publish to NPM/PyPI # This will publish your project on npm and PyPi once the above steps are done successfully by running these commands inside this step, if any error occurs then it should fail automatically🔄 https://github.com  // more options for actions can be provided in block using `run` keyword
        run: | # More details about each action will go here as per your requirements like publishing to npm or PyPI etc..⚠️, when not used explicitly but still needs a defined and also able specify the commands/actions within this step that should be executed in specific cases. In above scenario it would run `npm publish` for Publish packages on NPM OR use command similar syntax to make release using PyPI (pydistutils)
          echo "Publishing Python Application" # Echo is used instead of print statement so as not disturb GitHub's output and makes the process automated through commands, you provide all necessary details inside here. If any error occurs during this step then it should fail automatically🔄 https://github.com  // more options for actions can be provided in block using `run` keyword
      - name: Create Release # This will create a GitHub release with your build artifacts once the above steps are done successfully by running these commands inside here, if any error occurs then it should fail automatically🔄 https://github.com  // more options for actions can be provided in block using `run` keyword
        run: | # More details about each action will go within this as per your requirements like creating a release etc..⚠️, when not used explicitly but still needs to specified and able specify the commands/actions that should execute specifically under certain circumstances. In above scenario it would be using `github-release` package for releasing GitHub Release
          echo "Creating Python Application" # Echo is here instead of print statement so as make process automated through actions, provide all necessary details inside this block like name and body or any other required parameters to create release. If error occurs in these steps then it should fail automatically🔄 https://github.com  // more options for providing commands can be provided using `run` keyword
